title: 'Now only into writing fiction': Former Army chief Gen MM Naravane after uproar over unpublished memoir
summary_bullets: Former Army chief General MM Naravane announced a pivot to fiction writing following the release of his first book, 'The Cantonment Conspiracy'.
Naravane's upcoming memoir 'Four Stars of Destiny' recently sparked a major political firestorm in the Lok Sabha.
Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi used leaked excerpts from the unpublished memoir to criticize the PM's handling of the 2020 India-China border crisis, leading to intense parliamentary clashes.
blog_post: From the frontlines to the bookshelf, General MM Naravane is swapping military strategy for storytelling. After his unreleased memoir, 'Four Stars of Destiny', became the eye of a political storm in Parliament—prompting heated debates between Rahul Gandhi and the ruling government—the former Army chief says he is focusing exclusively on fiction. Having successfully navigated his debut with 'The Cantonment Conspiracy', Naravane is embracing his new identity as an author. It is a fascinating transition for a man who spent his life commanding troops, now finding himself at the center of the nation's most contentious political narratives.

title: PM Modi speaks with Bahrain King, condemns attacks on infrastructure, stresses secure shipping routes
summary_bullets: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and King Hamad Bin Isa Al Khalifa discussed the escalating instability in West Asia during a recent telephone call.
Both leaders expressed strong condemnation regarding attacks on critical energy and civilian infrastructure, noting their negative impact on global food and fuel supplies.
The conversation emphasized the urgent need to maintain secure and uninterrupted maritime shipping routes amidst rising regional tensions.
blog_post: Diplomacy takes center stage as PM Narendra Modi connects with Bahrain’s King Hamad Bin Isa Al Khalifa to address growing regional turbulence. Beyond the warm Eid al-Fitr greetings, the leaders tackled serious concerns regarding attacks on energy infrastructure that threaten global economic stability. By advocating for secure shipping lanes and freedom of navigation, India continues to position itself as a key voice for maritime security. Furthermore, the PM highlighted the welfare of the Indian diaspora in Bahrain, reinforcing the strong bilateral bonds between the two nations. It’s a timely reminder that global security starts with international dialogue.

title: Anganwadi workers to begin indefinite protest in Bengaluru from March 23
summary_bullets: The Karnataka State Anganwadi Workers’ Association is launching an indefinite protest in Bengaluru starting March 23 to demand better wages and improved service conditions.
Key demands include minimum wage status, social security benefits like PF and ESI, and the filling of numerous long-standing vacancies across the ICDS system.
Workers are also calling for better operational funding, infrastructure upgrades, and the rejection of the proposed direct benefit transfer (DBT) mechanism for food distribution.
blog_post: Anganwadi workers across Karnataka are taking a stand. On March 23, the State Anganwadi Workers’ Association will begin an indefinite protest in Bengaluru, highlighting a half-century of service under the ICDS that has yet to secure them fair pay or stable working conditions. From fighting for minimum wages and social security to demanding essential infrastructure and the removal of burdensome non-core duties, these frontline workers are calling for systemic change. As they push for better nutrition quality and a dedicated directorate, the government faces significant pressure to address these critical grievances. Stay tuned as this vital struggle for fair labor rights unfolds.

title: Nicobarese delegation meets LoP Rahul Gandhi, says tribal lands being encroached for mega project
summary_bullets: Nicobarese tribal leaders met with Rahul Gandhi to protest a ₹92,000 crore mega-infrastructure project encroaching on their ancestral lands.
The Tribal Council claims the government has ignored their repeated appeals and dismissed their revoked no-objection certificates for the project.
The community insists they are not anti-development but demand that construction be limited to government revenue land rather than their displaced tribal territories.
blog_post: A pivotal stand for land rights is unfolding in New Delhi. Tribal leaders from Great Nicobar Island have sought political intervention, meeting with Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi to highlight their struggle against a massive ₹92,000 crore infrastructure development. Since being displaced by the 2004 tsunami, the Nicobarese people feel their voices have been silenced by authorities. They aren't just fighting for soil; they are fighting for identity and survival. As the government pushes for a new transshipment port and township, the community’s plea is clear: true progress shouldn't come at the cost of ancestral heritage. Will their voices finally be heard?

title: Texelis, Scata team up on medium-heavy vehicle that can do drone defense
summary_bullets: French firm Texelis Defense and Finnish manufacturer Scata have partnered to develop the MK1, a new 18-ton multi-role combat vehicle.
The vehicle design focuses on lessons from the war in Ukraine, specifically incorporating enhanced top-side armor to defend against first-person-view drone attacks.
The MK1 is designed to fill an intermediate market gap, offering versatility for reconnaissance, troop transport, and counter-drone operations, with potential interest from Finland and Sweden.
blog_post: Modern warfare is evolving at a breakneck speed, and the new MK1 combat vehicle is proof of that shift. Developed in less than a year, this 18-ton machine is a direct response to the threats observed on the Ukrainian front lines. By prioritizing superior top-side protection against low-cost, high-impact drones, the partnership between Texelis Defense and Scata is bridging a critical gap in European defense. Whether it's troop transport or cutting-edge air defense, the MK1 is poised to become a versatile asset for European militaries. Keep your eyes peeled for its official debut at the Eurosatory arms fair this June!

title: Marines test ‘cruise control’ swim feature on amphibious vehicle prototype
summary_bullets: Marine Corps prototypes for the future Advanced Reconnaissance Vehicle (ARV) successfully completed initial water testing and firing drills at Camp Pendleton.
Companies Textron Systems and General Dynamics Land Systems are competing for the contract, with a final selection expected by 2030.
General Dynamics is testing a new 'autotrim' feature, similar to automotive lane-keeping, designed to help crews navigate water while focusing on combat tasks.
blog_post: The U.S. Marine Corps is stepping into the future of naval reconnaissance. Recent trials at Camp Pendleton have pushed prototypes for the new Advanced Reconnaissance Vehicle (ARV) to their limits, testing their ability to navigate turbulent waters while firing heavy weaponry. One standout innovation from General Dynamics is an 'autotrim' feature—essentially cruise control for the sea. By automating course corrections, this tech allows crews to prioritize mission objectives over driving mechanics. As we move toward 2030, these automated advancements are setting a new standard for amphibious mobility, proving that the battlefield of tomorrow is increasingly software-defined.
